zh.Pickwell, Leicestershire, England: 英國萊希斯特斯爾皮可維爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Eleanor Margaret de Grey於1539年出生在英國斯塔福爾恩維爾時，她的父親Thomas Grey29歲，母親Anne Verney25歲。她於1560年與William Anthony Cave在英國萊希斯特斯爾梅爾敦 莫布雷結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1591年8月17日在英國萊希斯特斯爾梅爾敦 莫布雷享年52歲，埋葬在英國。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Eleanor Margaret de Grey于1539年出生在英国斯塔福爾恩維爾时，她的父亲Thomas Grey29岁，母亲Anne Verney25岁。她于1560年与William Anthony Cave在英国萊希斯特斯爾梅爾敦 莫布雷结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于1591年8月17日在英国萊希斯特斯爾梅爾敦 莫布雷享年52岁，埋葬在英国。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Grey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: variant of   Gray  .
In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of Ukrainian Grushko: nickname or topographic name derived from a Russian-influenced transliteration of grushka ‘pear, pear tree’.

variants.name: Gray
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (especially Eastern Ulster; of Norman origin): habitational name from Graye in Calvados, France, named from the Gallo-Roman personal name Graec(i)us, meaning ‘Greek’ + the locative suffix -acum. This is probably the chief source of the surname in Britain.

English: nickname for someone with gray hair or a gray beard, from Middle English grey (Old English grǣg, grēg) ‘gray’. In Ireland it has been used as a translation of various Gaelic surnames derived from riabhach ‘brindled, gray’, including Mac Giolla Riabhaigh; see McGreevy . In North America, this surname has assimilated names with similar meaning from other languages.
French: habitational name from Gray in Haute-Saône or Le Gray in Seine-Maritime.

variants.name: Greiwe
meanings: German: possibly a nickname from Middle High German greibe ‘sharp, tangy, dour’. Compare Grey .

nameMeanings.name: Eleanor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Ellenor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor  , the result of blending with   Ellen .

variants.name: Elenor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Eleanor Margaret de Grey was born in 1539, in Enville, Staffordshire, England, her father, Thomas Grey, was 29 and her mother, Anne Verney, was 25. She married William Anthony Cave in 1560, in Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 6 daughters. She died on 17 August 1591, in Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, England, at the age of 52, and was buried in England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Eleanor Margaret de Grey est née en 1539, à Enville, Staffordshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Grey, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Anne Verney, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé William Anthony Cave en 1560, à Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée le 17 août 1591, à Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterrée à Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Eleanor Margaret de Grey 1539 in Enville, Staffordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas Grey 29 und ihre Mutter Anne Verney 25. Sie hat 1560 in Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, England William Anthony Cave geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb am 17. August 1591 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, England und wurde in England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Eleanor Margaret de Grey è nata il nel 1539 nel Enville, Staffordshire, England, sua padre, Thomas Grey, aveva 29 anni e sua madre, Anne Verney, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato William Anthony Cave nel 1560, nel Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 17 agosto 1591, nel Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, England, all'età di 52 anni, e fu sepolta a England.

ja.lifeSummary: Eleanor Margaret de Greyが1539年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアエンヴィルで生まれた時，父Thomas Greyは29歳，母Anne Verneyは25歳であった。1560年にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアメルトン モウブレイでWilliam Anthony Caveと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女は1591年8月17日にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアメルトン モウブレイで52歳で死去し，イギリスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Eleanor Margaret de Grey(이)가 1539년 영국 스태퍼드실르 빌에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Grey(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anne Verney(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1560년 영국 레이스스터실르 멜턴 모우브라이에서 William Anthony Cave(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1591년 8월 17일 영국 레이스스터실르 멜턴 모우브라이에서 52세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국에 묻혔다.

mn.Pickwell, Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire: Pickwell, Ratae Coritanorum, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Eleanor Margaret de Grey nasceu em 1539, em Enville, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Grey, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Anne Verney, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com William Anthony Cave em 1560, em Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 6 filhas. Ela faleceu em 17 de agosto de 1591, em Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, com 52 anos, e foi sepultada em Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Eleanor Margaret de Grey родилась в 1539 г. в местности энвилл, стаффордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas Grey было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Verney, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Anthony Cave в 1560 г. в местности мелтон мавбрэи, лисестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Она умерла 17 августа 1591 г. в местности мелтон мавбрэи, лисестиршир, Англия в возрасте 52 лет и была похоронена в местности Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Eleanor Margaret de Grey nació en 1539, en Enville, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Grey, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Anne Verney, tenía 25 años. Se casó con William Anthony Cave en 1560, en Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 17 de agosto de 1591, en Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 52 años, y fue sepultada en Inglaterra.
